other_observers: Solo, although it was seen yesterday and other days throughout the winter in this Pleasant Valley neighborhood.; Christy Pals first saw this bird in November at her feeder. She called her husband, Ken, who is a natrualist with the County Regional Park system and he didn't believe her, until he saw it himself, later on.
specific_location: The corner of 31 St. and 31st Circle in the Pleasant Valley neighborhood, adjacent to Rock Ledge Ranch, a section of Garden of the Gods.
description: This is a medium-sized, mostly red bird with a bushy red crest, an orange bill and a black eye, bib, and mask. It is the state bird of my home state, Ohio, in addition to the state bird of six other states. It is difficult to misidentify this species, especially the male.Behaviors: Singing from an exposed perch in a planted ornamental deciduous tree.Call: yes, both singing and calling. First heard call notes then I encouraged it to sing and it did.Plumage: alternate
